format: Brand collaboration — routine or tutorial, step by step with sensory detail
brief_snippet: Nykaa dermat-backed skincare collab featuring Bioderma, Cetaphil, Eucerin, ISDIN, Uriage for summer pigmentation
script:
Visual: Talking to the camera
PTC: It's 40 degrees outside… which means it's time to change your skincare with the season.

Visual: Touching skin + green screen showing concerns
PTC: Because as soon as it's Summer, my skin feels it before I do - it gets a little more dull, a little more uneven… and pigmentation shows up so much faster.

Visual: clean flatlay
VO: That's when I stop guessing… And to help me with all these concerns, I always trust Dermat backed skincare from Nykaa!
Super: Dermat Backed Skincare For Real Results

Visual: Bioderma cleanser application
Super: Bioderma Sebium Gel Moussant
VO: I start with the Bioderma Sebium Gel Moussant that cleans any buildup without making my skin feel stripped or tight…

Visual: Cetaphil serum application
Super: Cetaphil BHR Perfecting Serum
VO: Then I go in with the Cetaphil BHR Perfecting Serum to help improve overall skin texture and target early signs of pigmentation…

Visual: Eucerin serum
Super: Eucerin Anti-Pigment Dual Serum
VO: Followed by the Eucerin Anti-Pigment Dual Serum With Thiamidol & Hyaluronic Acid, which helps reduce dark spots and stops new ones from forming

Visual: ISDIN sunscreen application
Super: ISDIN Fotoprotector Fusion Water Magic SPF 50 PA++++
VO: …and of course, the ISDIN Fotoprotector Fusion Water sunscreen - especially in this heat - is something I don't skip at any cost.

Visual: Uriage sunscreen mist spray
Super: Uriage Bariesun Moisturizing Dry Touch Sunscreen Spray Mist SPF50+
VO: I also love topping up through the day with the Uriage Bariesun Dry Touch Sunscreen Spray - it's quick, lightweight, and super easy to reapply.

Visual: Skin shots
PTC: This just feels like skincare that actually understands what my skin needs right now.

Visual: aesthetic lineup / holding products
VO: You can find all of these dermat-approved picks on Nykaa
caption:
There's always a point where your skincare just stops keeping up.
Not because it's bad, but because the season has changed… and your routine hasn't.
Hotter days, more sun, more humidity, and suddenly your skin feels a little more dull, a little more uneven, and pigmentation starts showing up faster than usual.
I've learnt to take that as a cue to switch things up to choose skincare that actually works with the season, not against it.
And when it's dermat-backed, it just feels more intentional… like you're treating your skin, not guessing.
You can shop all my current picks on Nykaa at up to 15% off ✨
#Dermatapproved #Nykaa #Dermocosmetics #Ad

format: IMMBT — single product discovery

Didn't think much of it… until I tried it one day before stepping out.
It's the kind of blush that doesn't ask for attention. Just sits on the skin, blends in, and somehow you look a little more awake, a little more put together.
Been reaching for it more than I expected to.
#makeupfinds #everydaymakeup #beautyreels #Instagrammademebuythis @typsybeauty

format: Brand collaboration — personal narrative, emotional hook, product as solution
brief_snippet: SunScoop 5% Niacinamide Sunscreen Body Lotion - beach vacation, packing lighter
script:
Visual: Talking to the camera
PTC: I love beach vacations… but I hate the tan that comes with it.

Visual: Continue talking
PTC: And I used to carry both… body lotion and sunscreen. Until I realised I didn't need both.

Visual: Bring product in frame
VO: This is the SunScoop 5% Niacinamide Sunscreen Body Lotion

Visual: Applying on arms
VO: It moisturises like a body lotion, but also gives you SPF 50+ PA++++ protection from UVA and UVB rays.

Visual: Close-up texture, blending seamlessly
VO: It's super lightweight, absorbs really quickly, and there's no white cast.

Visual: Soft glow on skin + talking to camera
PTC: Plus, it's in-vivo tested and helps with tanning and uneven skin tone.

Visual: Talking to the camera with product in hand
VO: So now, I just carry one thing… and my skin's still taken care of. Packing lighter, but better.
caption: I used to think bodycare for vacations was just about hydration. SPF was always a

in my head - the rest, I'd figure out later (and regret later).
This summer, I've been doing it differently. Switched to the SunScoop 5% Niacinamide Sunscreen Body Lotion and it just fits in without feeling like an extra step. It's lightweight, no white cast, absorbs quickly - but also gives SPF 50+ PA++++ protection and helps with tanning and uneven skin tone. Plus, it's in-vivo tested, which makes it feel more reliable.
It's a small change, but it's made my routine feel a lot more complete.
#Ad #Sunscoop #SPFEveryday #SPFBodyLotion #NoMoreBodyTan @mynykaa

format: Brand collaboration — routine or tutorial, step by step with sensory detail
brief_snippet: Dyson Airstrait - Diwali hair styling tutorial, dries and straightens simultaneously, no heat plates
script:
Visual: Hair fully done, talking to the camera
PTC: This Diwali, I wanted my hair to look like I spent hours at the salon… but I didn't. Let me show you how I got this look at home.

Visual: Hair damp, bring Dyson in frame + product shots
VO: I'm starting with damp hair and using the Dyson Airstrait. It dries and straightens at the same time, which means no extra steps and no heat plates.

Visual: Turning it on, display lighting up
VO: It's so quick and smart, honestly my favorite thing to use when I'm getting ready for a busy festive day.

Visual: Styling roots first, then gliding through mid-lengths and ends
VO: I start at the roots to get rid of extra moisture, then just one smooth glide down the lengths for that sleek, frizz-free finish.

Visual: Fast-paced montage of styling one side and then transition
VO: It feels so light and looks super glossy. And the best part? My hair actually feels healthy even after styling - because the difference is Dyson.

Visual: Transition and show sleek straight hair
VO: Look at that shine - sleek, smooth, and ready in minutes. This is exactly why I love the Airstrait.

Visual: Quick hairstyle shot of putting the hairband and pinning the hair
VO: I'm just adding a simple festive touch with a hairband and some pins. It instantly makes the look Diwali-ready.

Visual: Final hair shots + product in hand closing
VO: And that's it - my easy, fuss-free Diwali hair look done in no time. Sleek, shiny, and healthy, all thanks to Dyson Airstrait.
caption:
This Diwali, I wanted my hair to shine as much as the diyas ✨
No salon, no stress - just me, my Dyson Airstrait, and a few minutes to go from damp to sleek, glossy, and festive-ready 🤍
Fast, fuss-free, and damage-free… that's when you really see the difference is Dyson 💕
#StyleWithDyson #DysonIndia #TheDifferenceIsDyson #Airstrait #FestiveHair #HealthyHair #DysonExperience #Collaboration @dyson_india @dysonbeauty

format: Event coverage — community or group event with friends
brief_snippet: Lakmé first-ever shade match session at Reliance Smart Bazaar - foundation, eyeshadow, lipstick, setting spray
script:
Visual: Outside the store, talking to the camera
PTC: I'm doing my first-ever meet and greet with some of you guys today at the Lakme Booth at the Reliance Smart Bazaar Full Paisa Wasool Sale, so come with me.

Visual: Walking inside. The Lakme booth comes into view. I start swatching products on my hand.
VO: I'm taking you through some of my favourite Lakme products that I genuinely love using, and am going to help you pick the right shade for you.

Visual: Foundation swatches on me + other people
VO: I love the Lakme 9 to 5 Hya Matte Foundation. I match it along the jawline so it blends into the face and neck. It feels lightweight, stays hydrating, and is easy to build.

Visual: Quick shots of Bejewel eyeshadow on lids
VO: For the eyes, I keep it really simple. With the Bejewel eyeshadows, I stick to one shade and blend it well so it looks soft and effortless.

Visual: Swatching the lipsticks
VO: For lips, I usually go for a nude, but with these, it's hard to choose because every shade looks good. Be it liquid, or bullets - they've got you covered!

Visual: Setting spray mist
VO: Everything gets sealed in with the Bejewel Setting Spray so the makeup stays put without feeling heavy.

Visual: Me talking to the group, explaining, smiling, interacting
VO: This shade match session was all about finding the right shade for you!

Visual: Wide shot of the group. I turn back to the camera with confidence.
VO: Thank you to everyone who came and spent their day with me. This was special and we're definitely doing more of this.
caption:
Doing my first-ever shade match session with some of you was honestly so special 🤍
From talking through an easy, everyday makeup routine to swatching my favourite products and answering all your questions in real time - this is exactly why I love beauty. It's simple, personal, and meant to be shared.
We did this during the Lakme sale at Reliance Smart Bazaar, using products I genuinely reach for - hydrating bases with hyaluronic acid, lightweight buildable coverage, comfy matte lips, and that final setting spray to lock everything in.
Thank you to everyone who came, listened, tried, and trusted me with their faces. This one felt like the beginning of something really nice.
#Lakme #LakmeIndia #LakmeMakeup #GoldenEventsale #Smartbazaar #ad

format: IMMBT — single product discovery

format: Brand collaboration — routine or tutorial, step by step with sensory detail
brief_snippet: Celimax The Vita-A Retinal Shot Tightening Booster - beginner retinal guide, dry skin application
script:
Visual: Talking to the camera
Super: Starting retinal? Save this.
VO: If you're new to retinal, this is exactly how I use the Celimax The Vita-A Retinal Shot Tightening Booster - without wrecking my skin.

Visual: Gently patting face dry with a towel
Super: Step 1: Dry skin only
VO: First things first - apply retinal only on completely dry skin. Even a little dampness can make retinal feel way harsher than it needs to.

Visual: Close-up of fingertip with one pea-sized amount
Super: Step 2: Amount matters
VO: With this retinal, beginners only need one pea-sized amount for the entire face. More isn't better - it's just more irritating.

Visual: Lightly touching areas like smile lines / fine lines
Super: Focus on concern areas
VO: I like to be intentional with where I place it — focusing on areas I'm actually targeting, like fine lines or smile lines, using the same small amount.

Visual: Dotting product on forehead, cheeks, chin
Super: Dot it out
VO: I always dot it out first so it spreads evenly and doesn't hit one area too hard.

Visual: Finished application
Super: Last step at night
VO: This one's designed to be the last step of your night routine, so no moisturiser after.

Visual: Morning light, applying sunscreen
Super: SPF is non-negotiable
VO: And the next morning - sunscreen. Always. No exceptions with retinal.

Visual: Skin shots
VO: Start once or twice a week, stay consistent, and let your skin adjust. Slow results are the ones that actually last.
caption:
Retinal taught me patience. Not overnight results. Not instant glow. Just quiet consistency.
Most irritation doesn't come from the ingredient - it comes from rushing it, overusing it, or trying to do too much.
This routine has been my reminder that skincare works best when it's simple, intentional, and respectful of your skin barrier.
If you're starting retinal, start slow. Your skin doesn't need pressure - it needs time.
Save this for the nights you're tempted to do 'just a little more.'
#Ad #Retinal @celimax.in @celimax.global

Your blush shouldn't fight your skin - it should follow your color theory
For the longest time, I thought some shades just

and some didn't… until I understood undertones. Warm, cool, neutral - once you know where you sit, everything starts to make sense. The way a shade blends, sits, even how it lifts your face
I've been reaching for the Maybelline New York Cloudtopia Cheek and Lip Mousse because it makes that so easy - soft, airy, almost cloud-like on the skin, but still pigmented. And using the same shade on cheeks and lips just pulls everything together

What's your undertone?
#MaybellineNewYork #Cloudtopia #ColorTheory

format: IMMBT — personal resistance resolved by the product
brief_snippet: Biodance Caviar PDRN mask - ₹2 lakh per kilo ingredient, hydrogel turns transparent as it absorbs
script:
Visual: Talking to the camera
PTC: This ingredient costs ₹2 lakh per kilo and Korean skin clinics have been using it for years, so when I saw it in a mask I was a little curious

Visual: Bring mask into frame
PTC: this is the Biodance Caviar PDRN mask

Visual: Opening the pack + texture shot of the mask
PTC: it has caviar for regeneration and PDRN which is usually something you hear about in clinic treatments

Visual: Applying mask
VO: but what I found interesting is the format because this isn't a regular sheet mask, it's a hydrogel that actually melts into your skin

Visual: Settling in
VO: so I just put it on and left it, no steps, no layering, nothing extra

Visual: Time passing
VO: and after a point it didn't even feel like I had anything on

Visual: Show the mask being transparent
VO: it literally starts turning transparent as it absorbs

Visual: Peel-off
VO: and once it's almost clear, that's when you take it off

Visual: Skin reveal
VO: my skin just looked more even, more fresh, like it had actually recovered from something

Visual: Final look
PTC: this is the kind of skincare that feels like it's actually doing the job
caption:
There's a reason Korean skin looks the way it does. And it's not always what we think.
I didn't expect to see caviar in skincare like this, but what really got me was PDRN - an ingredient that's been used in Korean skin clinics for years. What surprised me more was the format. It's not a regular sheet mask. It starts as a hydrogel and slowly turns transparent as it absorbs into your skin, so you can actually see it working.
I didn't layer anything with it or overcomplicate it. Just left it on and let it do its thing. And my skin felt more even, more rested… like it had actually recovered, not just looked better for a bit.
Not a trend. Just Korean skincare, done right.
#biodance #caviarPDRNmask #PDRNmask #koreanskincare #ad @biodance_in

caption:
Trying to make my hair behave after a trip is… a whole thing.
So I started carrying this tiny kit everywhere and honestly, it's taken the stress out of it. The shampoo resets, the mask softens and the serum just keeps everything protected and shiny.

format: Brand collaboration — personal narrative, emotional hook, product as solution
brief_snippet: Bare Anatomy Rosemary & Rice Water Hair Growth Spray - hair fall from city pollution and stress, spray at night
script:
Visual: Touching my hair while talking to the camera
PTC: Did you know the biggest causes of hair fall aren't what you think? And, it isn't just bad genes!

Visual: Pop up of images of pollution, stress, bad diets while I continue talking
PTC: It's not just one thing - city pollution, stress at work, skipping meals, and forgetting hair oil… it all adds up. Hair just doesn't get what it needs.

Visual: Bringing the Bare Anatomy Rosemary & Rice Water Hair Growth Spray in frame
PTC: That's when I realised I needed something simple - not another complicated routine. This is the Bare Anatomy Rosemary & Rice Water Hair Growth Spray.

Visual: Product shots
VO: It's made with 100% pure rosemary and rice extracts - no DIY mixing, no guesswork.

Visual: Spraying on scalp and light massage
VO: I usually use it at night - just spray, massage, and sleep. Rosemary helps reduce hair fall and improve thickness, while rice water conditions the scalp.

Visual: Fingers running through hair / hair shots
VO: It absorbs quickly, feels non-greasy, and leaves my hair softer, smoother, and lighter.

Visual: Talking to the camera with product in hand
PTC: If hair fall or thinning has been worrying you lately, start with consistency, not complexity. Sometimes, small daily habits make the biggest difference.
caption:
Hair fall used to feel like something I'd 'deal with later.' Until later started coming sooner than expected.
Somewhere between busy days and skipped routines, I realised my hair didn't need dramatic fixes - it needed quiet, everyday care. The kind you don't have to plan your life around.
Lately, I've been choosing consistency over chaos. One simple step at night, done regularly, and letting nature-backed ingredients do the slow work.
If you've been feeling that same worry creeping in, this is your reminder: start before it feels urgent 🤍
#BareAnatomy #HairCareJourney #ScalpCare #HairGrowthRoutine #Ad @mynykaa @bareanatomy.haircare

format: IMMBT — single product discovery
brief_snippet: Dove 10 in 1 Peptide Bond Strength Hair Mask - IMMBT version, protein peptide beads, low effort high impact
script:
Visual: Talking to camera, hair styled
PTC: This mask kept showing up on my feed… And every time, the claims sounded too good to be true.

Visual: Pick up the mask from my vanity
VO: Ten benefits in one. Bond repair. Low effort. Which immediately made me curious.

Visual: Talking to the camera with mask in my hand
PTC: Let's put this to the test. This is the Dove 10 in 1 Peptide Bond Strength Hair Mask.

Visual: 4 finger scooping shot + product texture
VO: What actually made me buy it were these protein peptide beads that melt into the hair to help repair damaged bonds.

Visual: Applying on damp hair, mid-lengths only
VO: I just apply it on damp hair, mid-lengths to ends, leave it on for a few minutes and rinse. That's it.

Visual: Hair open, natural movement
VO: Wait… look at that. It just feels softer - and there's way more shine than I expected.

Visual: Talking to the camera + product in hand
PTC: I didn't plan on buying another hair mask, but… this one got me. Especially for low-effort days. Added to my vanity!
caption:
January is when I consciously slow down. I don't want to do too much, but after all the festive heat, styling and colour, my hair definitely needs recovery.
This is where I keep things simple. The Dove 10 in 1 Peptide Bond Strength Hair Mask works deep inside the hair with its protein peptide beads that melt on application and help repair broken hair bonds caused by damage.
No long routines. Just stronger, smoother hair that actually feels healthier again.
If you're also treating January as a recovery season for your hair, this is an easy one to add.
#JanuaryReset #PostFestiveHair #HairBondRepair #DoveHairCare #Ad @dovechannelindia

format: Brand collaboration — personal narrative, emotional hook, product as solution
brief_snippet: Pond's Shaadi-Proof - wedding season, single woman at weddings, stopped shrinking, biome facewash + super light gel
script:
Visual: Talking to the camera + green screen visuals of me at various different weddings
PTC: You already know I've been a serial wedding attender. I've heard beta you're next more times than I can count.

Visual: Twirling in a lehenga
VO: I've attended enough weddings to know exactly how they go. The comparisons. The timelines. That little glance at your left hand before the question even comes.
But somewhere between outfit changes and unsolicited advice… something shifted.

Visual: More shots of me at a wedding / dancing at a sangeet
VO: I stopped shrinking. I stopped preparing answers in my head. And I started showing up… like I actually belong there.

Visual: Transition to present-day. I am getting ready for another wedding.
VO: These days when I get ready for a wedding, it's quieter in my head. No overthinking. No proving. Just prep.
And prep for me means keeping things simple. Because shaadi week is not the time to experiment with new actives and wake up with a breakout.

Visual: Using POND'S Biome Face Wash
VO: I use the Biome Face Wash. It's gentle, no tight dryness. Just clean, balanced skin. Because stressed skin + stressed mind? Not cute.

Visual: Applying Super Light Gel
VO: Then the Super Light Gel. Lightweight, absorbs fast, no chip-chip. Makeup sits beautifully on top. I glow, but I don't feel heavy.

Visual: Applying blue Body Lotion / adjusting sleeveless blouse
VO: And sleeveless blouse means body care too. This body lotion is light, soft, non-greasy so I'm not sticking to my own outfit mid-sangeet.

Visual: Close-up glow shot
VO: Different weddings. Different seasons. Same ease. Same glow
caption:
I've grown up at other people's weddings.
Different lehengas. Different phases of life. Same questions.
Somewhere between the sangeets and the subtle 'beta, you're next' smiles, I stopped feeling like I was being measured. I stopped shrinking to fit timelines that weren't mine. Showing up alone stopped feeling brave. It just started feeling normal.
Now when I get ready for a wedding, it's quieter in my head. A small ritual. A familiar glow. I've been reaching for Pond's Super Light Gel lately because it's lightweight, non-sticky, and packed with hyaluronic acid so my skin feels fresh and hydrated without feeling heavy under makeup. A reminder that I'm not late to anything - I'm exactly where I need to be.
Shaadi-proof doesn't mean unaffected. It just means unbothered. ✨
#ShaadiProof #OnMyTimeline #WeddingSeason #Ad @pondsindia
